_note: Reference values, TriskeleVM-style. EXACT match required. A count that DROPS is as suspicious as one that rises: either a deliberate fix (then --update-golden, and the drop is recorded under '_previous'/'_delta') or a validator that stopped biting. A silently shrinking count is the most dangerous signal in this repo — it looks like progress.

M3.note: No M3 checker. M3_Schema.shacl.ttl exists (2026-07-03, CONFORMS:True, 13 shapes) but no runner wraps it. An un-instrumented layer is not a passing layer — it is an unmeasured one.

M2.note: No M2 checker. The tscg-generate-mn-grammars skill can produce the SHACL; no runner yet.

M1.note: SC-6 BACKLOG (163 check_M1 errors) + SHACL DEBT (476 violations), both measured on the real repo. Both are EXPECTED to be non-zero. Lower them ONLY by repairing data or by fixing a SHACL bug — never by loosening a shape.

breakdown.DCC006: monoidal operator inside a signature — the bulk of SC-6
breakdown.DCC010: Fm1m2's first argument is not a registered Domain. FOUR PHANTOM DOMAINS: 'Music' (registry says MusicTheory), 'SystemicModeling' (registry says SystemsTheory), 'EnergyGenerators' (absent), 'Cascade' (not a domain at all — that Fm1m2 is an Fm2).
breakdown.GCC009/DCC009: guards appended after the signature. SCALAR guards = M0 measurements leaking into M1 → delete. QUALITATIVE guards (trajectoryShape=Circular) = real semantics → DO NOT DELETE, pending D11/SC-3.
breakdown.DCC008: Amplifier: nested parentheses + `Ft`, which is not a TSCG primitive at all
breakdown.CTX001: 3 WARNINGS (not errors): M1_Domains lacks the 'm2' key in its @context; M1_BusinessModeling lacks 'm3:eagle_eye' and 'm3:sphinx_eye'. Counted in `warnings: 3`, and therefore in by_code too — by_code counts ALL codes emitted, warnings included. Omitting them from the golden set made the gate fail on a reference that was itself incomplete.

M1._delta_reason: SC-1.5, 2026-07-14: M1_CoreConcepts v2.8.0 — @context coercion ("rdfs:subClassOf"/"rdfs:domain"/"rdfs:range": {"@type":"@id"}). Its 26 violations were ALL 'rdfs:subClassOf must point to ...', caused by IRIs serialised as string literals. The file is now the ONLY SHACL-conformant M1 file. VERIFIED TARGETED: the other 15 files' counts are IDENTICAL to the digit (46·2·4·30·58·42·104·6·18·32·36·24·20·24·30). Same fix made M1_CoreConcepts reasonable by Pellet for the first time — it had been UNANALYSABLE, all 19 subClassOf axioms silently ignored.

M0.note: check_m0_instances.py runs; golden counts not yet captured. Run --update-golden once, REVIEW the numbers, then trust them.

_known_pre_existing_not_counted_here.SHAPE2_vs_SHAPE3: ~256 of the 502 SHACL violations are a VALIDATOR BUG, not data defects. sh:targetClass follows subclasses, so every extension combo is validated TWICE — by SHAPE 2 (which demands Fm2( ) and SHAPE 3 (which demands Fm1m2( ) — mutually exclusive. No extension combo can satisfy both. SHAPE 2 must exclude the subclass. → SC-5. THIS IS THE REAL GOLDMINE: ~256 violations that describe no defect at all.
_known_pre_existing_not_counted_here.phantom_knowledgeField: ~77 violations: m2:knowledgeField is a phantom property (never declared in M2) → SC-5 (Domain fusion).
_known_pre_existing_not_counted_here.CORRECTED_2026-07-14: An earlier claim — repeated several times and taken from the 2026-07-03 handover WITHOUT VERIFICATION — held that ONE @context line ("rdfs:subClassOf":{"@type":"@id"}) would dissolve ~500 SHACL violations. THAT WAS WRONG. Only M1_CoreConcepts had the literal-serialisation defect; the 15 extensions already used {"@id": ...} and pass Pellet. Fixing it did NOT drop the SHACL count — but it took M1_CoreConcepts from UNREASONABLE (Pellet crashed, all 19 subClassOf axioms ignored, class hierarchy invisible) to logically consistent. The gain was existential, not quantitative: the layer could have been wrong with no way to find out.
_known_pre_existing_not_counted_here.invalid_URI_warning: '<path> does not look like a valid URI' on all 16 files: rdflib builds the base IRI from the WINDOWS PATH instead of the declared @base. Every M1 IRI is therefore minted under E:\... and does not match the IRIs M2/M3 use. Fix is in the script (pass publicID= to rdflib.parse), not in the data. May account for some of the remaining violations — UNVERIFIED.
